summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a near‑four‑hour London show featuring monster Truckin’/The Other One marathon and a soaring Morning Dew, making it one of the top non‑Dark Star performances of 1972.
review: This recording delivers crystal‑clear, well‑balanced sound from the Betty Cantor/Rex Jackson 24‑track board, preserving every nuance of what many fans call a “must‑have” Europe ’72 show.  The band is on fire – the second set is an epic jam suite anchored by Truckin’ → The Other One → Morning Dew, while the first set packs in classics like Playin' in the Band and Dire Wolf.  Overall the performance ranks among the very best of the year, with a warm tube‑amp vibe that showcases Jerry’s lead work, Keith’s piano magic, and Bill’s percussive power.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen reviews (most 5‑star) praise both the musical performance and the soundboard quality; only one low‑rating comment is about pricing, not content.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, warm tube‑amp tone from a custom 24‑track mobile truck (Betty Cantor & Rex Jackson).  No noticeable dropouts or muffiness; reviewers repeatedly call the mix “balanced”, “great sound quality”, and “exceptionally good”.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A marathon ~4‑hour set (first set >2 hrs) that builds momentum into a second‑set jam suite.  The first half is solid, the second half is an uninterrupted journey of Truckin' → The Other One (twice) → Morning Dew with superb transitions and dynamic peaks.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead guitar tone is described as “perfect”, “locked in” with Keith, and a driving force throughout the long jams.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass solo before The Other One is highlighted; his foundation is called “solid” and “tight”.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocal on Sing Me Back Home and his classic “short shorts/Turlock” banter are praised; rhythm work noted as strong.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux receives glowing remarks for piano fluidity, counter‑point to Garcia, and being an essential part of the jam chemistry.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drum solo is singled out as a highlight; his percussive drive fuels The Other One sections.
